title: Extreme heat wave in Japan causes one death and dozens of hospitalizations
summary: High temperatures have caused at least one death from heatstroke, hundreds of hospitalizations, and the rescheduling of events.

title: Nurse accused of murdering patient by injecting feces into intravenous drip
summary: The suspect, a former nurse at the hospital, allegedly introduced fecal matter into the drip, causing the patient's death.

title: Japan approves law penalizing destruction of national flag
summary: The legislation, which imposes fines for damaging or removing the Rising Sun flag, was approved amid criticism from the opposition.

title: Susumu Tonegawa, Japanese Nobel Prize winner in Medicine, dies at 86
summary: The scientist won the Nobel for his discoveries on the genetic diversity of antibodies, revolutionizing immunology.

title: Japanese parliament reforms Imperial House Law to allow princesses to retain status after marriage
summary: The amendment seeks to ensure imperial succession by allowing princesses to maintain their place in the royal family even after marriage.

title: Yomiuri Giants and SoftBank Hawks win in Japanese professional baseball day
summary: In the games of July 16, the Giants defeated the Yakult Swallows and the Hawks defeated the Rakuten Eagles.

title: Japanese media recall the 2022 World Cup final with analysis of Argentina's defense and Messi's performance
summary: Various articles highlight Argentina's defensive solidity, the viral photo of the captains, and Messi's decisive goal.

title: Japan comes back against Canada to achieve its tenth consecutive victory in the Volleyball Nations League
summary: The Japanese team, with outstanding performances from its players, overcame an 0-2 deficit to win 3-2.

title: Ukraine replaces its Defense Minister amid public discontent
summary: The change in defense leadership occurs in the context of the war with Russia and generates criticism.

title: More than 500 Rohingya die in shipwreck off the coast of Myanmar
summary: The vessel carrying members of the Rohingya minority sank, and it is feared that all have perished.

title: Support for Prime Minister Takaichi's cabinet falls to 49%, the lowest level since its formation
summary: A public opinion poll reveals that government approval has dropped below 50% for the first time.

title: NVIDIA will supply next-generation systems for the development of Japanese artificial intelligence
summary: The US company will provide its latest chips to boost the national AI project 'Noetra'.

title: Japanese government proposes new economic benefit system linked to personal income
summary: The new program, to be implemented in 2029, seeks to replace the current reduction in consumption taxes.

title: Government decides on Kyoto route for extension of Hokuriku bullet train
summary: The new line will pass through JR Katsuragawa Station in Kyoto, raising concerns among the local community.

title: New plaque placed at monument to victims of attack on Yamayuri center in Sagamihara
summary: 10 years after the tragic event, the name of another victim was added to the commemorative plaque.

title: Osaka high school baseball tournament advances with resounding victories
summary: Riseisha and Osaka Toin achieve quick victories, while Doshisha Kori scores 25 runs.

title: Chudō party leader harshly criticizes government management in parliamentary session
summary: In an interview, representative Ogawa accused the ruling party of not meeting democratic standards in the Diet.

title: Chairman of the Expo 2025 exhibition committee resigns after scandal over unsafe electric buses
summary: The resignation comes after it was revealed that the safety risks of the vehicles were not properly assessed.

title: Two children drown in separate incidents in rivers in Gifu and Kochi
summary: A junior high school student and an 11-year-old boy died while bathing in rivers in their respective prefectures.

title: US economic indicators show moderate growth in June
summary: Retail sales increased slightly and the Federal Reserve reports a gradual expansion in most of its districts.
